headline: Corning (GLW) Lands Amazon Fiber Deal As AI Data Center Demand Builds
summary: Corning (NYSE:GLW) has signed a major multiyear fiber optics supply agreement with Amazon. The deal ties Corning directly to U.S. data center expansion and AI infrastructure buildout. Corning is also working with companies such as Nvidia and Meta and ramping manufacturing capacity for optical products. For investors watching Corning, the new Amazon agreement comes alongside a sharp move in the stock. NYSE:GLW last closed at $205.83 and is up 17.3% over the past week, 6.1% over the past...

headline: Rebound in tech shares pushes Asian shares higher, while oil prices fall
summary: Shares were mostly higher Thursday in Asia, led by tech-driven gains in Japan and South Korea as major computer chipmakers’ stocks surged following upbeat earnings reports from U.S. giants like Qualcomm and Micron Technology.  Oil prices fell more than $1, bringing them closer to where they were before the war with Iran started.  Qualcomm’s share price surged 12% in afterhours trading after the company announced it had raised its forecast for revenue this year to $40 billion from $22 billion.

content.title: Rebound in tech shares pushes Asian shares higher, while oil prices fall
content.summary: Shares were mostly higher Thursday in Asia, led by tech-driven gains in Japan and South Korea as major computer chipmakers’ stocks surged following upbeat earnings reports from U.S. giants like Qualcomm and Micron Technology.  Oil prices fell more than $1, bringing them closer to where they were before the war with Iran started.  Qualcomm’s share price surged 12% in afterhours trading after the company announced it had raised its forecast for revenue this year to $40 billion from $22 billion.

thumbnail.caption: People walk past a monitor showing Japan's Nikkei 225 index at a securities firm in Tokyo, Tuesday, June 23, 2026. (AP Photo/Hiro Komae)

headline: WhatsApp's pick of Indian fintech founder signals scale of payment ambitions
summary: Kunal Shah, an Indian fintech founder with no engineering degree or Silicon Valley pedigree, has spent two decades building businesses around digital payments and consumer behaviour in India.  He now takes charge of Meta's WhatsApp as the company looks to ‌leverage the reach of the world's largest messaging platform and build a "superapp" to capture a bigger share of surging online payments, industry players say.  Meta was looking for ‌a leader with "an intuitive grasp of the immense, global product potential for WhatsApp", Chief Product Officer Chris Cox wrote in an internal memo announcing 47-year-old Shah's appointment on Monday.

content.title: WhatsApp's pick of Indian fintech founder signals scale of payment ambitions
content.summary: Kunal Shah, an Indian fintech founder with no engineering degree or Silicon Valley pedigree, has spent two decades building businesses around digital payments and consumer behaviour in India.  He now takes charge of Meta's WhatsApp as the company looks to ‌leverage the reach of the world's largest messaging platform and build a "superapp" to capture a bigger share of surging online payments, industry players say.  Meta was looking for ‌a leader with "an intuitive grasp of the immense, global product potential for WhatsApp", Chief Product Officer Chris Cox wrote in an internal memo announcing 47-year-old Shah's appointment on Monday.

headline: Rebound in tech shares pushes world markets higher, while oil prices fall
summary: World shares were mostly higher Thursday, led by tech-driven gains in Japan and South Korea as major computer chipmakers’ stocks surged following upbeat earnings reports from U.S. giants like Qualcomm and Micron Technology.  Oil prices slipped closer to where they were before the war with Iran began.  Qualcomm’s share price surged 11.5% in pre-market trading after the company announced it had raised its forecast for revenue this year to $40 billion from $22 billion.

headline: Global chip stocks surge as blowout Micron results reignite AI rally
summary: Global chip stocks surged on Thursday after Micron Technology's blockbuster results reignited the AI-driven rally, as investors grew more confident about persistent demand and tightening supply. Micron, a key supplier for Nvidia's AI chipsets ‌alongside South Korean chipmakers, surged more than 17% in U.S. premarket trading after it forecast quarterly profit and revenue well above expectations. The ‌company, whose shares have more than tripled in value so far this year, also said its customers had committed $22 billion to lock in supplies of memory chips, underscoring how AI-driven ​demand is tightening the market.

content.title: Global chip stocks surge as blowout Micron results reignite AI rally
content.summary: Global chip stocks surged on Thursday after Micron Technology's blockbuster results reignited the AI-driven rally, as investors grew more confident about persistent demand and tightening supply. Micron, a key supplier for Nvidia's AI chipsets ‌alongside South Korean chipmakers, surged more than 17% in U.S. premarket trading after it forecast quarterly profit and revenue well above expectations. The ‌company, whose shares have more than tripled in value so far this year, also said its customers had committed $22 billion to lock in supplies of memory chips, underscoring how AI-driven ​demand is tightening the market.

thumbnail.caption: FILE PHOTO: Illustration shows computer motherboard and chip
